metadata.title: College Student Car Accidents: Back-to-School Risks | Bull Attorneys
metadata.description: Car accidents spike as college students return to school. Learn top risks, prevention tips, and how Bull Attorneys can help after a student car accident.

hero.title: College Student Accidents Returning to School This Time of Year

sections.content: An annual return to school that proves deadly when it comes to parties, alcohol and other factors on the road for students. College student accidents, and any car related injury accident is often overlooked in the excitement of going back to school.

Students that are beginning to go to a new college or returning to their existing college can often have car accidents because of the excitement of going to college. Too often we look past the cause of college student accidents and this time of year is especially dangerous. Unfortunately, with college comes learning to consume alcohol and attending parties. Many students end up driving after attending a college party and the drunk driving leads to horrific accidents.

You can teach your kids to learn about how to protect themselves and others from having car accidents which lead to severe injuries or wrongful death. It is extremely important to train and educate your young driving students. Read through these thoughts and teach your young student how to avoid bad car accidents.

sections.heading: Why is returning to college campuses a dangerous time for car accidents?
list: Your child may be eager to return to campus and want to drive a long distance by themself when returning to college. Many other younger drivers are out on the road and returning to campuses at the same time. These students are not yet completely experienced safe driving and fatigue.
Many high school and college students will drive in the dark at a time when it is dangerous to be on highways. Statistics prove that more car accidents happen at night.

Students may be hanging out to say goodbye to their close friends before they leave to go back to campus and stay up late partying with friends before driving the next day when they are tired or hungover which causes their alertness to be highly reduced.

sections.heading: When is your child most at risk for having a car accident when returning to college?
list: When driving in the dark and nighttime conditions.
When driving long distance driving to a university far away from home.
When driving alone without a companion to help keep them alert, awake and safe.
After staying up too late with friends before leaving to school.
College student accidents regularly have alcohol involved.

sections.heading: How can you protect your child from having a car accident when returning to school?
list: Make sure your child has plenty of rest the night before they drive back to college, student accidents happen annually.
Talk to your child about the dangers of driving when tired so they understand that fatigue is one of the leading causes of car accidents.
Teach your child that they should never use their phone while driving and have them use the various apps that do not allow them to text while driving.
If they are going on a long distance trip to return to college travel with them or make arrangements for them to have a safe friend to travel along with them.
Provide assurance that it is okay to stop at a motel and spend the money for sleep instead of trying to make the trip in one day.
Have them check in with you every hour or two to talk to them and find their location and distance driven.
Ship their car from your home to the school and travel together with them.

sections.heading: What should you do when your child has a car accident a long way from home while traveling back to college?
list: Have your child trained to immediately call 911 followed by calling you to notify you of the location and time of the accident.
Locate the nearest major hospital and call 911 yourself to communicate with them about the severity of the injuries.
Tell your child not to give any statements to insurance adjusters or investigators.
Immediately retain an experienced car accident attorney like us.
Tell your child to get photos of the cars and accident scene if possible as well as photos of all injuries.
